title: Where would I travel if I could travel again - Request

enrichment.summary: Kyle responds to a viewer request asking where he would travel if he could go anywhere. He shares that he no longer has a strong desire to travel, but if he had money he'd take his mom to Scotland (Edinburgh, Glasgow, and Kyle of Lochalsh for ancestral roots) and Rome, where his deep knowledge of Roman history would make it meaningful. He reflects on his past travels, including a five-and-a-half month trip in 2010, and explains his current philosophy of 'creating multiple Boulders' — building rich inner/local worlds rather than physically traveling.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: no longer motivated to travel; prefers staying in Boulder
would take mom to Scotland (ancestral connection) and Rome (deep historical knowledge)
found extended travel disorienting — got 'spun out' after too long away
prefers creating rich parallel realities locally rather than physically going places
new technologies like VR and GoPro footage let him 'travel' without leaving his room
